title: About
text:
Sebastian Jaramillo
I am a Ph.D. candidate in Mathematics at the University of Oregon. My research lies at the intersection of probability, Markov chain Monte Carlo methods, and the mathematical analysis of sampling algorithms.
Alongside my doctoral research, I have developed a strong applied interest in time series, econometrics, causal inference, machine learning, and quantitative finance. I am currently building a technical portfolio that translates my mathematical training into applied projects relevant for industry roles.
My long-term goal is to work on quantitative problems where rigorous mathematical thinking, statistical modeling, and computational implementation meet.

title: Projects
text:
This portfolio connects my mathematical training with applied work in time series, econometrics, causal inference, machine learning, and quantitative finance.
Each project includes code, documentation, and a short explanation of the modeling problem, methodology, and results.

title: Projects
text:
Featured Portfolio

Time Series Portfolio
A unified collection of applied time series projects covering Bayesian modeling, forecasting, dynamic regression, vector autoregressions, and macroeconomic applications.
Repository: Time-Series
Planned modules:

Project 01: Bayesian Modeling for Binary Time Series

Project 02: Forecasting and Dynamic Time Series Models

Project 03: Vector Autoregressions and Impulse Responses

Project 04: Bayesian VARs and Macroeconomic Applications




Econometrics and Causal Inference
Applied projects involving regression, instrumental variables, regression discontinuity, matching, bootstrap inference, and causal interpretation.
Topics include:

Fuzzy regression discontinuity

Treatment effect estimation

Bootstrap uncertainty

Propensity score methods

Bayesian causal forests




Quantitative Finance
Projects connecting stochastic modeling, time series, and financial applications.
Topics include:

Portfolio construction

Hedging strategies

Volatility modeling

Risk analysis

Forecasting financial time series




Machine Learning
Machine learning and deep learning projects with emphasis on mathematical modeling, uncertainty, and applied prediction.
Topics include:

Predictive modeling

Model evaluation

Computer vision

Deep learning

Uncertainty quantification

title: Research
text: My doctoral research focuses on probability and the mathematical analysis of Markov chain Monte Carlo algorithms.

title: Research
section: Hamiltonian Monte Carlo and Sampling Algorithms
text:
Hamiltonian Monte Carlo and Sampling Algorithms
My current research studies Hamiltonian Monte Carlo-type algorithms, coupling methods, and convergence behavior for probability distributions with geometric structure.
I am particularly interested in how algorithmic design choices affect mixing, stability, contraction, and convergence rates.

title: Research
text:
Selected Research Themes

Hamiltonian Monte Carlo

Coupling and path coupling

Mixing times

Wasserstein contraction

Sampling on manifolds

Strongly log-concave targets

Non-standard kinetic energies

Quantitative convergence bounds

title: Research
text:
Research-to-Industry Connection
Although my research is theoretical, the ideas connect naturally to computational statistics, Bayesian inference, simulation, uncertainty quantification, and scalable probabilistic modeling.
